title: Learning Without Critics? Revisiting GRPO in Classical Reinforcement Learning Environments
imageCaption: Performance comparison of PPO and GRPO across classical RL environments (CartPole, Acrobot, HalfCheetah, Humanoid, MountainCarContinuous), showing that relative performance is highly context-dependent.

title: Do Reasoning Models Ask Better Questions? A Formal Information-Theoretic Analysis on Multi-Turn LLM Games
imageCaption: Framework architecture for the multi-turn LLM information-seeking evaluation, showing the flow between Seeker, Oracle, and Pruner agents in a hierarchical knowledge graph environment.

title: Partial Reasoning in Language Models: Search and Refinement Guided by Uncertainty
imageCaption: Overview of the PREGU architecture: the LLM generates partial reasoning paths in token space, halts when entropy exceeds threshold, then refines in latent space via Soft Reasoning to select the best answer.

title: CONV-TO-BENCH: Evaluating Language Models via User–Assistant Dialogues in Code Tasks
Personalizing Fairness: Adaptive RL with User Diversity Preference for Recommender Systems
imageCaption: A2Fair framework figure from the paper showing the adaptive RL approach for personalized fairness in recommender systems.

title: InfoQuest: Evaluating Multi-Turn Dialogue Agents for Open-Ended Conversations with Hidden Context
imageCaption: InfoQuest's three-stage benchmark construction process: initial state generation, user setting with persona/goals/constraints, and checklist generation for evaluating information gathering.

title: Sliding Puzzles Gym: A Scalable Benchmark for State Representation in Visual Reinforcement Learning
imageCaption: Overview of SPGym: the framework extends the 8-tile puzzle by replacing numbered tiles with image patches, sampling from a pool to scale visual representation complexity while keeping environment dynamics fixed.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Debt Pricing: A Case Study in Financial Services
imageCaption: Traffic percentage figure from the paper showing the deployment results of the RL-based debt pricing system in production at a large financial institution.

title: PulseRL: Enabling Offline Reinforcement Learning for Digital Marketing Systems via Conservative Q-Learning
imageCaption: PulseRL system architecture: the end-to-end offline RL pipeline for communication channel optimization in Digital Marketing Systems, including data processing, CQL training, deployment, and monitoring.
